title: Bash One-Liner Patterns
when_to_use: When automating repetitive recon or analysis tasks during engagements

steps.title: Search-Sort-Deduplicate
steps.instruction: cat file | grep 'term' | sort | uniq — search, sort, deduplicate
steps.tip: sort must come before uniq — uniq only removes adjacent duplicates

steps.title: Search Across Files
steps.instruction: find . -name '*.log' | xargs grep 'error' — search across files
steps.tip: xargs converts stdin to arguments. Use -print0 and -0 for filenames with spaces

steps.title: Extract Port Numbers
steps.instruction: nmap output | grep 'open' | cut -d'/' -f1 — extract open port numbers
steps.tip: cut splits on delimiter (-d) and selects field (-f). Powerful for structured output parsing

steps.title: Append to File
steps.tip: >> appends. > overwrites. Always use >> when building results over time

steps.title: Chain on Success
steps.tip: && only runs command2 if command1 succeeds. Perfect for dependent operations

steps.title: Chain on Failure
steps.tip: || runs command2 only if command1 fails. Useful for fallback commands

steps.title: Loop Through File
steps.instruction: for i in $(cat list.txt); do echo $i; done — loop through a file
steps.tip: Replace echo with any command: nmap, whois, dig. Automate repetitive tasks
